name: ERNIE 5.0 (Preview)
archDisplay: Natively omni-modal Mixture-of-Experts, unified autoregressive next-token objective, <3% of experts active per token
note: 2.4T-parameter natively omni-modal flagship unveiled at Baidu World 2025; scored 22 on Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index as a free preview.

results.notes: IFBench, from AA's Intelligence Evaluations breakdown for 'ERNIE 5.0 Thinking Preview'.
MMMU-Pro, from the same AA breakdown.

LMArena Text leaderboard Elo score for the ERNIE-5.0-Preview-1203 checkpoint (posted by the official @arena/LMArena account), a +23pt update over the earlier -1103 checkpoint; ranked #1 among Chinese models and #2 globally on the Text leaderboard at the time. Corroborated by Baidu's own ERNIE blog post (https://ernie.baidu.com/blog/posts/ernie-5.0-preview-1203-release-on-lmarena/). Not previously tracked -- existing results for this model (gpqa, hle, ifbench, mmmu-pro) are all from artificialanalysis.ai's Intelligence Evaluations breakdown; this is a genuinely new benchmark/slug (LMArena Elo) from a genuinely new, independent source.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: unveiled Nov 13 2025 at Baidu World per PRNewswire/winbuzzer/SCMP, 2.4T params with <3% experts active per inference matches winbuzzer. GPQA Diamond=78 and HLE=13 both literally confirmed on the artificialanalysis.ai page (ERNIE 5.0 Thinking Preview row in the evaluation breakdown charts). Free ($0/$0) preview pricing also confirmed on that page.

2026-07-16 gap-fill round (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu — all 7 remaining core-benchmark gaps checked in one pass, all resolve to omitted_no_evidence): (1) Baidu's own Nov 13 2025 launch materials for ERNIE 5.0 (Preview) presented benchmark comparisons only as un-transcribed chart images at the Baidu World event -- confirmed by direct fetch of winbuzzer.com, aigazine.com and related launch coverage, one of which states explicitly "Baidu did not release full benchmark details or raw scores publicly, and the results are unpublished beyond screenshots, so reproducibility is impossible." Qualitative claims only ("topped charts across knowledge, reasoning, math, coding"); no numeric MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU values in any text-extractable source. ernie.baidu.com itself is unreachable from this environment (DNS ENOTFOUND), consistent with prior rounds' findings for sibling ERNIE models. (2) Independent leaderboards: artificialanalysis.ai's own page for "ERNIE 5.0 Thinking Preview" (the source already used for this model's gpqa/hle/ifbench/mmmu-pro entries) was fetched directly (both via WebFetch and a raw curl of the full HTML/embedded JSON) -- its current Intelligence Index v4.1 methodology is explicitly documented on-page as 9 evaluations (GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR), which does NOT include MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, or MMMU; the per-model detail charts are client-side-rendered and no numeric values for these 7 slugs could be recovered from the static payload. Epoch AI's raw benchmark feed (epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json, downloaded and grepped directly) contains zero rows mentioning "ernie" or "baidu" in any form -- Epoch does not track any Baidu/ERNIE model at all. Vals.ai and SuperCLUE searches surfaced no ERNIE 5.0 (Preview) numeric scores on these 7 benchmarks (SuperCLUE search returned only LMArena-Elo-related coverage, already tracked via the arena slug). CodeSOTA's ERNIE 5.0 model card has exactly one benchmark on file (GSM8K, not a tracked core slug), dated 2026-03-01 and of unclear checkpoint. (3) Sibling-model cross-reference: ERNIE X1's corpus notes already establish zero benchmark data exists for that sibling. A Feb 4 2026 arXiv paper, "ERNIE 5.0 Technical Report" (arxiv.org/abs/2602.04705), DOES report numeric scores for a model it calls plainly "ERNIE 5.0" (Table 1/2: MMLU 90.58, MMLU-Pro 83.80, MATH(CoT) 73.89, AIME 2025 89.06, LiveCodeBench v6 76.21, HumanEval+ 94.48; no SWE-bench or plain-MMMU rows found) -- but this is confirmed via llm-stats.com and independent Chinese-press coverage (blog.csdn.net, 36kr-linked pieces) to describe Baidu's January 22, 2026 full/GA release of ERNIE 5.0, a later, separate model milestone from the November 13, 2025 "(Preview)" checkpoint this corpus entry tracks. The one benchmark both eras nominally share, GPQA-Diamond, diverges sharply (86.36 in the Feb 2026 report's post-trained table vs. this entry's existing AA-sourced 78 for the Preview specifically), confirming these are materially different checkpoints with several months of additional post-training between them -- the Technical Report's numbers were therefore deliberately NOT attributed to this Preview entry to avoid misrepresenting the earlier checkpoint's capability. (Separately noted for any future GA-model corpus entry: this arXiv report would be the primary source for that model's mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/livecodebench gaps, but that is out of scope here since no "ERNIE 5.0" (non-preview) entry currently exists in corpus/models/baidu/.) A third-party aggregator (franklineh.com) shows plausible-looking AIME-2025 (~85%), MMLU-Pro (~83%), and LiveCodeBench (~81.2%) figures explicitly labeled "ERNIE 5.0 Thinking Preview" that are consistent in style with this entry's existing AA-sourced GPQA/HLE/IFBench values, but these could not be independently corroborated against artificialanalysis.ai's own primary page/data within this round (chart data is JS-rendered and not present in the static payload) and are not one of this corpus's three prescribed source types, so they were not added -- flagged here as a lead for a future round with better AA-scraping tooling. Conclusion: mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, and mmmu remain genuine data gaps for ERNIE 5.0 (Preview) specifically -- Baidu's preview-stage launch simply did not publish machine-readable scores on these benchmarks, and no independent tracker has filled the gap for this exact checkpoint as of this round.

2026-07-18 round (livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu -- the 3 gaps from this pass, all resolve to omitted_no_evidence, no additions): Re-confirmed vision capability first (modalities includes \"vision\", capabilities.vision=true) so MMMU is NOT architecturally inapplicable here -- it is a genuine unpublished-data gap like the other two, not a modality mismatch (mmmu-pro is already tracked at 65 for this model; plain MMMU is a distinct, still-missing slug). Checked 5 of 6 prescribed methods: (1) Official launch materials: ernie.baidu.com remains unreachable from this environment (getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND), consistent with every prior round. (2) Independent trackers -- fetched artificialanalysis.ai's own model page for \"ERNIE 5.0 Thinking Preview\" plus four of its head-to-head comparison pages (vs GPT-5.1 Codex high, vs Kimi K2.5, vs GLM-5, vs Qwen3-235B): AA's structured evaluation tables explicitly render \"No data available\" for LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench/SWE-bench Verified, and MMMU for this model -- i.e. AA itself confirms it does not hold these three values, not merely that its Intelligence Index excludes them. Checked LiveCodeBench's own official leaderboard (livecodebench.github.io/leaderboard.html) and SWE-bench's own official leaderboard (swebench.com) directly -- neither lists any Baidu/ERNIE entry at all. Checked llm-stats.com's dedicated SWE-bench Verified and MMMU leaderboard pages directly (104 and 63 models respectively) -- no ERNIE row on either. Checked BenchLM.ai's MMMU benchmark page -- 9 models listed, none from Baidu. A third-party aggregator, franklineh.com, again surfaces a LiveCodeBench=81.2% figure labeled \"ERNIE 5.0 Thinking Preview\" (same number flagged as an uncorroborated lead in the 2026-07-16 round); this round specifically checked whether AA (the presumptive origin of franklineh's data) actually carries that number, and confirmed AA's own comparison-page tables say \"No data available\" for this exact model/benchmark pair -- meaning franklineh's figure is NOT traceable to AA and has no visible independent methodology page (franklineh.com/about 404s). Given the corpus's requirement for a real, verifiable citation and this being the second round to flag the same number as unverifiable, it is deliberately still not added. llmbase.ai's comparison page returned HTTP 403 (blocked, could not check). Vals.ai and Scale SEAL (labs.scale.com/leaderboard) searches surfaced no ERNIE 5.0 entries on either platform's public leaderboards. (3) Academic: re-fetched the arXiv \"ERNIE 5.0 Technical Report\" (2602.04705) and specifically searched its full text for any mention of \"Preview\" or a November 2025 checkpoint -- found none; the paper does not distinguish itself from an earlier preview at all, reinforcing the 2026-07-16 finding that it describes solely the January 22, 2026 GA release, a separate checkpoint from this Preview entry (already evidenced by the diverging GPQA-Diamond values, 78 vs 86.36). The report does reference an MMMU row in its section 6.2 vision-benchmark table but the number is for the GA model, not this Preview checkpoint, and was not extracted or used for that reason. No SWE-bench/SWE-bench Verified mention found anywhere in the report. (5) Sibling-model cross-reference: broad web searches for ERNIE 5.0 Preview's coding/agentic benchmarks surfaced only TAU2-Bench, ACEBench, BFCL v4, BrowseComp, SpreadSheetBench, LiveCodeBench v6, HumanEval+, MBPP+, and CRUXEval as benchmarks Baidu evaluates ERNIE 5.0 on in some capacity, per secondary summaries of the GA-era technical report -- none of these secondary sources supplied a citable numeric LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU score specifically attributed to the November 2025 Preview checkpoint. Conclusion: livecodebench, swe-bench, and mmmu remain genuine data gaps for ERNIE 5.0 (Preview) specifically as of this round -- confirmed via direct inspection of both benchmarks' own official leaderboards (zero Baidu/ERNIE presence) plus AA's own structured tables affirmatively showing no data held for this model on any of the three, going beyond the 2026-07-16 round's methodology-page-only finding.
